QQQ_vol_carry_R2sizing.note: de Prado CDF x inverse-vol overlay; sizing reverted (Calmar regressed)

QQQ_vol_always_long_R2sizing.note: IDENTICAL to carry under R2 sizing => outcome driven by vol-overlay, not labeler

confirmed: ROUND1: on GLD and QQQ (both vol and dollar axes), EVERY labeler (carry, kmeans2stage, hmm) collapses to EXACTLY the always_long buy-and-hold result (1 trade, identical Calmar/DA). Replicated 2 tickers x 2 axes.
ROUND1: n_cells=5-9 per train run => featured labelers DID fit and save non-degenerate cells. The collapse is NOT a labeling failure.

ROUND1 ROOT CAUSE: the bottleneck is MODULE 8 (sizing/threshold), NOT axis or labeling. Infer rule w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) saturates to full-long when p>tau+0.005; isotonic-calibrated probs sit far above the low fixed tau (0.35 GLD / 0.15 QQQ) for the whole OOS window => pinned 100% long => 1 trade == buy-and-hold for every cell.
ROUND1: hmm baseline == always_long on both assets (no separation).

ROUND1 CONFIRM: a NEW featured labeler (trend_leg = forward-drawdown leg labels, K=1.0 sigma band, horizon selected on TRAIN balance) was added and run on GLD vol + QQQ vol. It ALSO tied buy-and-hold EXACTLY (GLD 1.6165/24.0211/1-trade; QQQ 1.1099/22.8298/1-trade == always_long). This is independent confirmation that the bottleneck is module 8 sizing, NOT the labeler: a better label cannot express itself while w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) pins 100% long. The labeler was reverted (no OOS lift over baseline).

ROUND2: DE-SATURATION CONFIRMED. Replaced w=min(1,(p-tau)*200) with de Prado CDF bet w=clip(2*Phi((p-.5)/sqrt(p(1-p)))-1,0,1) * inverse-vol overlay clip(std_100/std_20,0.25,1.0), applied IDENTICALLY in trainer.realistic_cstats (VAL synth) and templates/infer.py.tmpl (OOS), causal (vol from past bar returns only). QQQ vol: trades 1->431, DA 22.83->7.07 (-69%), but REAL Calmar 1.11->0.54 (HALVED). The bottleneck is broken (trades>>1, DA crushed) but the overlay OVER-DE-LEVERS on a drift asset: MaxDD shrank, CAGR shrank MORE, so Calmar fell below buy-hold. Reverted (Calmar regressed).

ROUND2: carry == always_long to 4 dp under the new sizing (both 0.5387 / 7.0697 / 431 trades). The outcome is driven ENTIRELY by the vol-targeting overlay, INDEPENDENT of the labeler (a real AUC-0.80 model and a constant-long signal give the identical exposure path). Re-confirms R1: the labeler is not the lever; module 8 is.

ROUND2: the new VAL synth_cal = -0.3511 CORRECTLY predicted OOS underperformance (R1 synth_cal was a flat uninformative 0.0). Restoring train/infer sizing consistency restored a usable in-sample selection signal.
dead_ends: ROUND1: sweeping axis (vol vs dollar) and labeler (carry/kmeans2stage/hmm) with the CURRENT sizing is futile -- all 16 cells tie buy-and-hold. Do NOT keep iterating on axis/label choice until module 8 sizing de-saturates.

ROUND1: adding the trend_leg labeler alone (no sizing change) does NOT de-saturate -- it ties buy-and-hold like every other labeler. Reverted. A new labeler is only worth testing AFTER module 8 sizing genuinely modulates exposure on p; bring trend_leg BACK in round 2+ paired with the de-saturated sizing rule.

ROUND2: a CDF map centered at p=0.5 + an aggressive inverse-vol overlay (floor 0.25, fast=20/slow=100) is TOO AGGRESSIVE for drift assets -- it cuts CAGR faster than MaxDD and halves Calmar. Do NOT use floor 0.25 / center-0.5 again. The DA win (-69%) is real but useless if CAGR collapses. The fix is to STAY INVESTED BY DEFAULT and only de-lever in genuine turbulence.
next_idea: ROUND3: KEEP the de-saturating sizing structure (it works: trades 1->431, DA -69%) but DE-TUNE the de-levering so CAGR recovers toward buy-hold. (a) Raise VOL_FLOOR 0.25->0.6 and trigger only on large spikes (e.g. fast=10/slow=60, clip(slow/fast,0.6,1.0)) so normal regimes hold ~full exposure. (b) Recenter the CDF on tau not 0.5: map conviction c=(p-tau)/(1-tau) through the CDF so the low per-ETF tau keeps drift assets long instead of the implicit p>0.5 gate flattening half the time. Target on QQQ vol: trades>>1 AND DA<22.83 AND Calmar>=1.11 (>= buy-hold). Then replicate on GLD vol and add TLT negative control. Use the now-informative VAL synth_cal to pre-screen before spending QC backtests.
frontier: QQQ on vol axis remains the frontier. ROUND2 proved the sizing is the lever (trades 1->431, DA -69% achievable) -- now recover CAGR by de-levering only in turbulence (raise floor, recenter CDF on tau).
notes: clean slate 2026-06-01. R1 = landscape + bottleneck = module 8 sizing. R2 = de-saturated sizing (de Prado CDF x inverse-vol): broke the 1-trade collapse (431 trades) and crushed DA -69% on QQQ vol, but over-de-levered => Calmar 1.11->0.54, DISCARDED & reverted. Best REAL Calmar still buy-hold (QQQ 1.11, GLD 1.62). The R2 mechanism (carry==always_long) re-confirms labeler is not the lever; sizing is. VAL synth_cal now tracks OOS sign (was 0.0).
